topic: A person runs into their ex at a mutual friend's wedding
raw_output:
[story 1]

The ballroom air buzzed with the kind of celebratory noise that always made my chest feel light. I’d spent the last six months meticulously building this new life—career-wise, personally, everything—and tonight felt like the culmination of every hard-won victory. Then, across the glittering expanse of crystal and laughter, I saw him. Liam. He was standing near the punch bowl, looking effortlessly charming, and for a split second, the carefully constructed calm I’d cultivated fractured. My smile, which had been perfectly calibrated for the toasts, hitched. I watched him turn, his eyes sweeping the room, and for a breath, I felt a sharp, undeniable sense of having navigated a difficult landscape and emerged victorious. I took a slow sip of champagne, letting the warmth settle in my core, and decided I would greet him with an easy, unruffled nod.

[story 2]

I leaned against a marble pillar, taking in the spectacle of the wedding—the silk, the expensive wine, the sheer joy radiating off everyone. It was a performance, and I was the lead actor, basking in the glow of my own success. Then, a familiar silhouette caught my peripheral vision. It was Sarah. We hadn't spoken in years, and the air immediately thickened. My jaw tightened almost imperceptibly. When our eyes met across the crowded room, there was a flicker—a recognition that settled deep in my gut like a well-placed anchor. I straightened my posture, letting my gaze linger just a moment longer than necessary. A small, knowing smirk played on my lips as I turned back to my conversation, feeling an undeniable sense of having successfully outmaneuvered whatever had once held me back.

[story 3]

The music swelled, a vibrant wave washing over the reception hall. I felt a profound sense of settling into my own skin tonight; the confidence was a tangible thing, humming just beneath my surface. I was here, thriving, and everything I had fought for was visible in the sparkle of the chandeliers. Then, I spotted him near the entrance, exchanging a warm greeting with a mutual acquaintance. It was him. A strange, warm current shot through me, momentarily disrupting the steady rhythm of my evening. I watched them for a moment, a subtle shift in my expression—a softening around the eyes that spoke volumes. I pushed off the wall, walking toward the edge of the dance floor, a steady, unhurried pace, feeling entirely in command of the space around me.
